summary: Applied ntopng live on the Proxmox host with direct bridge capture on vmbr10 and vmbr0 plus an operator-only Tailscale proxy on port 3001.

verification.observed: redis-server and ntopng were both active, ntopng listened on 127.0.0.1:3000, and the operator-facing proxy listened only on 100.118.189.95:3001 on tailscale0.

verification.check: Operator API over the Tailscale path
verification.observed: The proxied interfaces endpoint at http://100.118.189.95:3001/lua/rest/v2/get/ntopng/interfaces.lua authenticated with the managed admin password and returned both vmbr0 and vmbr10.
After generating probe traffic to 10.10.10.10, the vmbr10 interface API reported visible packets, bytes, flows, and hosts, including 283 active flows and 28 hosts at the time of verification.

verification.check: Top hosts on the private bridge
verification.observed: The vmbr10 top-hosts API returned active talkers including 10.10.10.1, 10.10.10.20, 10.10.10.50, grafana.lv3.org, and 10.10.10.30.

notes: The first implementation keeps ntopng on the Proxmox host because ntopng itself is not a NetFlow collector and the main requirement was direct visibility into vmbr10.
The operator path intentionally stays private on the Tailscale address and is not published through DNS or the public NGINX edge.
